 {"id":"bd-x4x7","title":"Task: Implement agent lifecycle event dispatch (agent_start/agent_end)","description":"# Task: Implement Agent Lifecycle Event Dispatch

## Objective

Dispatch agent_start and agent_end events at the beginning and end of agent runs.

## Background

Agent lifecycle events allow extensions to:
- Initialize resources at agent start
- Clean up resources at agent end
- Log overall agent activity
- Modify system prompts (agent_start)

## TypeScript Reference

```typescript
// At start of agent.run()
if (runner.hasHandlers('agent_start')) {
    await runner.emit({
        type: 'agent_start',
        sessionId: session.id,
    });
}

// At end of agent.run() (including errors)
if (runner.hasHandlers('agent_end')) {
    await runner.emit({
        type: 'agent_end',
        sessionId: session.id,
        messages: conversation.messages,
        error: error?.message,
    });
}
```

## Implementation

### In Agent Run Method (src/agent.rs)

```rust
impl Agent {
    pub async fn run(&mut self) -> Result<AgentResult> {
        // Get event dispatcher if extensions are loaded
        let event_dispatcher = self.extension_manager
            .as_ref()
            .map(|em| em.event_dispatcher());
        
        // Dispatch agent_start
        if let Some(dispatcher) = &event_dispatcher {
            if dispatcher.has_handlers("agent_start") {
                let event = ExtensionEvent::AgentStart {
                    session_id: self.session.id().to_string(),
                };
                dispatcher.dispatch::<()>(event).await?;
            }
        }
        
        // Run agent loop
        let result = self.run_loop(&event_dispatcher).await;
        
        // Dispatch agent_end (even on error)
        if let Some(dispatcher) = &event_dispatcher {
            if dispatcher.has_handlers("agent_end") {
                let event = ExtensionEvent::AgentEnd {
                    session_id: self.session.id().to_string(),
                    messages: self.session.get_messages(),
                    error: result.as_ref().err().map(|e| e.to_string()),
                };
                let _ = dispatcher.dispatch::<()>(event).await;
                // Ignore dispatch errors during cleanup
            }
        }
        
        result
    }
}
```

## Event Data

### agent_start
```json
{
    "type": "agent_start",
    "sessionId": "abc-123"
}
```

### agent_end
```json
{
    "type": "agent_end",
    "sessionId": "abc-123",
    "messages": [...],
    "error": null
}
```

### agent_end (with error)
```json
{
    "type": "agent_end",
    "sessionId": "abc-123",
    "messages": [...],
    "error": "Rate limit exceeded"
}
```

## Handler Examples

```javascript
// Track agent sessions
ctx.on('agent_start', (event) => {
    analytics.track('agent_start', { sessionId: event.sessionId });
});

ctx.on('agent_end', (event) => {
    analytics.track('agent_end', {
        sessionId: event.sessionId,
        messageCount: event.messages.length,
        hadError: \!\!event.error,
    });
    
    if (event.error) {
        errorReporter.report(event.error);
    }
});
```

## Important Considerations

### agent_end Must Always Fire

Even if the agent loop errors or panics, agent_end should fire:

```rust
// Use a guard or finally pattern
struct AgentEndGuard<'a> {
    dispatcher: Option<&'a EventDispatcher>,
    session_id: String,
    fired: bool,
}

impl<'a> Drop for AgentEndGuard<'a> {
    fn drop(&mut self) {
        if \!self.fired {
            if let Some(dispatcher) = self.dispatcher {
```
